title: ficus hispida var. hispida
intro: Ficus hispida var. hispida, also known as the bengkung or bengkung tree, is a species of fig tree native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, hairy or hispid leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ficus hispida var. hispida include reducing inflammation, alleviating fever, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional remedies. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is often used in combination with other herbs in herbal preparations to enhance its medicinal effects.
